steps.title: Identify Parallel Lines Condition
steps.content: For a system to have no solution, the equations must represent parallel lines, meaning the coefficients of $x$ and $y$ are proportional but the constants are not. Check if each system satisfies this condition.

steps.title: Analyze Choices for Proportionality
steps.content:
Choice D: $-2x + 3y = 9$ and $4x - 6y = 18$
- Multiply first equation by $-2$: $4x - 6y = -18$
- Second equation is $4x - 6y = 18$.
Left sides are identical (proportional coefficients), but constants differ ($-18 ≠ 18$). This confirms parallel lines.

steps.title: Eliminate Other Choices
steps.content:
Choice A: Equations are identical (multiply first by -1). Infinite solutions.
Choice B: Coefficients are not proportional. One solution.
Choice C: Second equation is -3 times the first. Infinite solutions.
Only D has no solution.
